english: down, as soon as an opportunity offers, S.g., and hence źe-sdáṅ dam-ríṅ (zhe sdang dam ring) a long lingering, lurking grudge S.g.

pf. °das ('das) (prob. vb. n. to bdá-ba (bda' ba), déd-pa (ded pa)) to pass over, 1. to travel over, to clear a certain space, thaṅ de (thang de) this plain Sambh.; źag dú-mai lam (zhag du ma'i lam) (to perform) many day's journeys Dzl. - 2. c. las (las): to go beyond, to surpass Dzl.; lhá-las dás-pai spos (lha las 'das pa'i spos) incense surpassing that of the gods, i.e. that which is burnt to them S.O.; to exceed, tsád-las (tshad las) the measure Lt.; gráṅs-las dás-pa (grangs las 'das pa) Tar. surpassing number, innumerable; bsám-byai yúl-las (bsam bya'i yul las) (surpassing) the understanding or imagination, inconceivable Glr.; to transgress, to trespass against, bká-las (bka' las), krims-las (khrims las), a commandment, a law = gál-ba ('gal ba); to get over a thing, to get the better of, to overcome, = rgyál-ba (rgyal ba); to go away from, mya-ṅán-las (mya ngan las) q.v.; to let go, leave off, abandon, čós-las (chos las) one's religion Thgy.; bló-las dás-pa? (blo las 'das pa?) - 3. with or without dús-las (dus las), tse (tshe), resp. sku (sku), to depart this life, to die; das-po ('das po) the deceased, defunct, late, Lex.; *de-lóg* (de log) W. the soul of a deceased person, ghost, apparition; the re-appearing is possible only for about forty days after death, as long as the Bardo lasts, v. bar-do (bar do). - 4. to pass by, = to disappear, nyi-zlá °dás-nas (nyi zla 'das nas) when the sun and the moon have disappeared (for a time); very frq. relative to time: to pass away, to elapse, °dás-pai dus ('das pa'i dus) the time that has passed, is gone, past time, v. dus (dus) 5.; zla dgu °dás-nas (zla dgu 'das nas) after nine months Lt.; °dus-ló (dus lo) the year past, °das-zlá ('das zla) the month past, °das-źág ('das zhag) the day past; *°de-źág-la* (de zhag la) W. the other day, lately; nyin-mtsán čós-kyis °dá-bar bya (nyin mtshan chos kyis 'da' bar bya) day and night are spent in religious exercises; dgé-bai byá-ba kó-nas dus °da (dge ba'i bya ba kho nas dus 'da') Tar. (time) spent in none but works of virtue. — °dá-ga (-ma) ('da' ga (-ma)) Cs. hour of death, °da-ga-ye-śés ('da' ga ye shes) knowledge of the hour of death (title of a book).

to tremble, shudder, shiver, quake, gráṅ-bas °dár-ba (grang bas 'dar ba) to shiver with cold; ǰígs-pas (jigs pas) (to tremble) with fear; °dár-źiṅ °gúl-ba ('dar zhing 'gul ba) id.; °dár-bar °gyúr-ba ('dar bar gyur ba) to begin to tremble; °dar-yám (dar yam) Sch. doubting, wavering, undetermined, °dar-yám byéd-pa (dar yam byed pa) to doubt, to waver.
ru-°dál (ru 'dal), ru-°drél (ru drel) a single horn Sch. - bad-°dál (bad 'dal) prov., being left exhausted on the road, sinking under fatigue.
= dál-ba (dal ba), ču-°dál (chu 'dal) still water Lex.

demonstr. pron. this, ṅai bu °di (nga'i bu 'di) this my son; ṅai °di (nga'i 'di) this of me, i.e. that which I am doing just now Glr., what I am experiencing just now Mil.; the present, the respective, grúb-pa-po °di (grub pa po 'di) the respective performer (of an incantation) Dom.; such a one, bdag miṅ °di źes-byá-ba (bdag ming 'di zhes bya ba) 1, such and such a one Thgr., also °di daṅ °di ('di dang 'di) (-lta-bu (-lta bu)) and similar expressions, ṅas kyód-la °di daṅ °di-lta-bu źig sbyín-no (ngas khyod la 'di dang 'di lta bu zhig sbyin no) I give you such and such a thing. On the difference between °di ('di) and de (de) v. de (de); the plural forms and derivatives of both of them are in conformity; only the following may be particularly mentioned: °di-ka-ráṅ ('di ka rang) is used also for just here, just now Mil.; °di-lta-ste ('di lta ste) for instance, to wit, such as, viz.; also pleon. with źé-na (zhe na): gnyis gaṅ źé-na °di-lta-ste (gnyis gang zhe na 'di lta ste) Wdn.; čí pyir źé-na °di-lta-ste (ci phyir zhe na 'di lta ste) Pth.; °di-ltar ('di ltar) so, in this manner, číi pyir kyod °di-ltar gyur (ci'i phyir khyod 'di ltar gyur) in what manner have you become so, how did you get into this condition? Dzl. frq.; °di-ltar-ro ('di ltar ro) it ran thus, it was to this effect, of this purport Glr. frq.; ṅa °di-ltar yin (nga 'di ltar yin) such I am, I am, live, go, just as you see me here Mil.; in the verse: dus-byas čos-rnams °di-ltar blta ('dus byas chos rnams 'di ltar blta) 'compounded things must be regarded thus' - the word °di-ltar ('di ltar) is meant to be accompanied by a snap of the fingers (se-gól (se gol), or skád-čig-ma (skad cig ma)); °di-nas ('di nas) from this place, from this time present, as yet, still. °di (daṅ) pyí (-ma) ('di (dang) phyi (-ma)) the present and the future life, frq.; °di pyíd sdéb-pa ('di phyid sdeb pa), rjé-ba (rje ba) to exchange this life for the future one, i.e. tse pyí-ma blós-btaṅ-ste °dii don sgrúb-pa (tshe phyi ma blos btang ste 'di'i don sgrub pa) to be earthly minded C.; *°di-zug* ('di zug), *i-zug* (i zug) W., so, thus; *°di-ríṅ* ('di ring) W. to-day; °di-ru ('di ru) (come) in here, into this place; here, at this place, frq.; now, seldom.
